prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(register_loan_case primary_party_1)
(assign_approval_resource_to_case primary_party_1 approval_resource_slot_1)
(attach_pricing_model_to_case primary_party_1 pricing_model_standard_index)
(confirm_pricing_and_initiate_structuring primary_party_1 pricing_model_standard_index)
(release_pricing_model_from_case primary_party_1 pricing_model_standard_index)
(attach_pricing_model_to_case primary_party_1 pricing_model_standard_index)
(provisionally_assign_primary_exposure_profile primary_party_1 exposure_profile_primary_standard pricing_model_standard_index)
(release_pricing_model_from_case primary_party_1 pricing_model_standard_index)
(bind_product_term_to_case primary_party_1 product_term_template_standard_amortization)
(confirm_primary_exposure_profile primary_party_1 exposure_profile_primary_standard product_term_template_standard_amortization)
(release_product_term_from_case primary_party_1 product_term_template_standard_amortization)
(register_loan_case secondary_party_1)
(assign_approval_resource_to_case secondary_party_1 approval_resource_slot_2)
(attach_pricing_model_to_case secondary_party_1 pricing_model_standard_index)
(confirm_pricing_and_initiate_structuring secondary_party_1 pricing_model_standard_index)
(release_pricing_model_from_case secondary_party_1 pricing_model_standard_index)
(attach_pricing_model_to_case secondary_party_1 pricing_model_standard_index)
(provisionally_assign_secondary_exposure_profile secondary_party_1 exposure_profile_secondary_standard pricing_model_standard_index)
(release_pricing_model_from_case secondary_party_1 pricing_model_standard_index)
(bind_product_term_to_case secondary_party_1 product_term_template_standard_amortization)
(confirm_secondary_exposure_profile secondary_party_1 exposure_profile_secondary_standard product_term_template_standard_amortization)
(release_product_term_from_case secondary_party_1 product_term_template_standard_amortization)
(assemble_loan_offer_package primary_party_1 secondary_party_1 exposure_profile_primary_standard exposure_profile_secondary_standard loan_offer_package_slot_1)
(attach_pricing_model_to_case primary_party_1 pricing_model_standard_index)
(finalize_loan_offer_package loan_offer_package_slot_1 primary_party_1 pricing_model_standard_index)
(approve_primary_party_for_execution primary_party_1 loan_offer_package_slot_1)
(approve_secondary_party_for_execution secondary_party_1 loan_offer_package_slot_1)
(authorize_activation_and_assign_funding primary_party_1 funding_profile_standard pricing_model_standard_index)
(activate_offer_for_primary_party primary_party_1 approval_resource_slot_1 funding_profile_standard)
(release_pricing_model_from_case primary_party_1 pricing_model_standard_index)
(attach_pricing_model_to_case secondary_party_1 pricing_model_standard_index)
(authorize_activation_and_assign_funding secondary_party_1 funding_profile_standard pricing_model_standard_index)
(activate_offer_for_secondary_party secondary_party_1 approval_resource_slot_2 funding_profile_standard)
(release_pricing_model_from_case secondary_party_1 pricing_model_standard_index)
(register_loan_case structuring_instance_1)
(assign_approval_resource_to_case structuring_instance_1 approval_resource_slot_1)
(attach_pricing_model_to_case structuring_instance_1 pricing_model_standard_index)
(confirm_pricing_and_initiate_structuring structuring_instance_1 pricing_model_standard_index)
(release_pricing_model_from_case structuring_instance_1 pricing_model_standard_index)
(instantiate_and_bind_security_document structuring_instance_1 security_doc_template_mortgage_deed loan_offer_package_slot_1)
(attach_pricing_model_to_case structuring_instance_1 pricing_model_standard_index)
(validate_documents_and_mark_bound structuring_instance_1 security_doc_template_mortgage_deed loan_offer_package_slot_1 pricing_model_standard_index)
(attach_pricing_adjustment_to_structuring_instance structuring_instance_1 pricing_adjustment_override_1)
(bind_product_term_to_case structuring_instance_1 product_term_template_standard_amortization)
(apply_pricing_adjustment_primary_path structuring_instance_1 pricing_adjustment_override_1 product_term_template_standard_amortization security_doc_template_mortgage_deed loan_offer_package_slot_1)
(attach_external_approval_token structuring_instance_1 external_approval_record_1)
(external_approval_without_tranche_flags structuring_instance_1 external_approval_record_1 security_doc_template_mortgage_deed loan_offer_package_slot_1)
(finalize_structuring_instance_without_execution_method structuring_instance_1)
(authorize_activation_and_assign_funding structuring_instance_1 funding_profile_standard pricing_model_standard_index)
(activate_offer_structuring_instance structuring_instance_1 approval_resource_slot_1 funding_profile_standard)
(release_pricing_model_from_case structuring_instance_1 pricing_model_standard_index)
(release_product_term_from_case structuring_instance_1 product_term_template_standard_amortization)
(release_pricing_adjustment_from_structuring_instance structuring_instance_1 pricing_adjustment_override_1)
(release_external_approval_token structuring_instance_1 external_approval_record_1)
(register_loan_case structuring_instance_2)
(assign_approval_resource_to_case structuring_instance_2 approval_resource_slot_1)
(attach_pricing_model_to_case structuring_instance_2 pricing_model_standard_index)
(confirm_pricing_and_initiate_structuring structuring_instance_2 pricing_model_standard_index)
(release_pricing_model_from_case structuring_instance_2 pricing_model_standard_index)
(instantiate_and_bind_security_document structuring_instance_2 security_doc_template_guarantee loan_offer_package_slot_1)
(attach_pricing_model_to_case structuring_instance_2 pricing_model_standard_index)
(validate_documents_and_mark_bound structuring_instance_2 security_doc_template_guarantee loan_offer_package_slot_1 pricing_model_standard_index)
(attach_pricing_adjustment_to_structuring_instance structuring_instance_2 pricing_adjustment_override_1)
(bind_product_term_to_case structuring_instance_2 product_term_template_standard_amortization)
(apply_pricing_adjustment_primary_path structuring_instance_2 pricing_adjustment_override_1 product_term_template_standard_amortization security_doc_template_guarantee loan_offer_package_slot_1)
(attach_external_approval_token structuring_instance_2 external_approval_record_1)
(external_approval_without_tranche_flags structuring_instance_2 external_approval_record_1 security_doc_template_guarantee loan_offer_package_slot_1)
(finalize_structuring_instance_without_execution_method structuring_instance_2)
(authorize_activation_and_assign_funding structuring_instance_2 funding_profile_standard pricing_model_standard_index)
(activate_offer_structuring_instance structuring_instance_2 approval_resource_slot_1 funding_profile_standard)
```

## Explanation of Generated Plan
The plan systematically processes each entity (primary_party_1, secondary_party_1, structuring_instance_1, and structuring_instance_2) to achieve `offer_activated` status, reusing limited resources like pricing_model_standard_index, funding_profile
message.reasoning:
The problem is complex with many objects and actions. The goal is to activate offers for primary_party_1, secondary_party_1, structuring_instance_1, and structuring_instance_2.


## Breaking Down the Problem  
- The task involves managing multiple entities like primary_party_1, secondary_party_1, structuring_instance_1, and structuring_instance_2.  
- The goal is to activate offers for these entities, indicating a need for coordinated actions.  
- The complexity arises from handling interactions between these objects, requiring careful planning.  

## Defining Activation Goals  
- Activation likely means enabling or triggering offers for each entity.  
- The process may involve sequential or simultaneous actions, depending on dependencies.  
- Understanding initial conditions is key to mapping out the activation steps.

## Outlining Activation Steps  
- Activating offers for entities involves registering a loan case, assigning approval resources, and attaching a pricing model.  
- The process includes confirming pricing, initiating structuring, and ensuring readiness for execution.  

## Managing Entity-Specific Actions  
- Primary_party_1 and secondary_party_1 relate to borrower roles, while structuring_instances connect to offer roles, all linked to a loan case.  
- Exposure and collateral actions are specific to parties, with steps like primary_exposure_profile_assigned ensuring alignment with the loan case.  

## Finalizing Offer Activation  
- Offers for primary_party_1, secondary_party_1, and structuring_instances require separate activation actions like activate_offer_for_primary_party.  
- Activation needs authorization, approved resources, and funding, with the final step setting offer_activated and releasing resources.  

## Achieving Readiness for Execution  
- Entities can become ready for execution via methods like finalizing structuring instances, with or without execution methods, or after external approval.  
- For primary parties, readiness also requires committed exposure and finalized package allocation, triggering ready_for_execution_flag_for_entity.

## Assembling Loan Offer Packages  
- Assembling a loan offer package requires primary and secondary parties to be ready, with various committed or provisional states for exposure and parties.  
- There are four assemble actions: standard (no tranche flags, provisional exposures, committed parties), mark_primary_tranche (committed primary exposure, provisional secondary), mark_secondary_tranche (provisional primary, committed secondary exposure), and mark_both (committed both exposures, not committed parties).  

## Finalizing Packages and Documents  
- Finalizing a loan offer package needs pricing attached to the primary party, with allocated resources and readiness conditions met.  
- Security documents are instantiated and bound to the package after structuring is initiated, requiring available security templates and finalized package allocation.  

## Managing Structuring Instances  
- Structuring instances involve a sequence of attaching pricing adjustments, external approval tokens, and binding product terms to the loan case.  
- Finalizing structuring instances can occur after external approval or with/without execution methods, depending on tranche flags and document binding.  

## Handling Document Validation  
- Documents are validated and marked bound if instantiated, with pricing attached, and no primary tranche flag, ensuring document readiness.  
- Alternatively, acknowledging consent binds documents for packages with a primary tranche flag, requiring consent attachment and acknowledgment.  

## Applying Pricing Adjustments  
- Pricing adjustments are applied via primary or secondary paths, depending on tranche flags, after documents are bound and adjustments are attached.  
- The process requires product terms and security documents to be in place, committing the adjustment for further steps.

## Registering and Initiating Cases  
- Both primary and secondary parties can register loan cases and assign approval resources, starting the structuring process.  
- Pricing models are attached to cases, confirmed, and later released, ensuring both parties are initiated for further steps.  

## Preparing Primary Exposure  
- Primary exposure readiness requires provisional assignment with pricing or collateral attachment, ensuring structuring is initiated.  
- Binding product terms to the case and confirming exposure profiles sets primary_exposure_committed, making the party ready for offer assembly.  

## Managing Exposure States  
- Exposure can be provisional or committed, with distinct predicates like exposure_provisional_primary and exposure_committed_primary for tracking.  
- Finalizing primary exposure and collateral commits the party's exposure, updating states to support package assembly requirements.

## Preparing Exposure Profiles  
- Primary and secondary parties provisionally assign exposure profiles, attaching pricing models like pricing_model_standard_index.  
- Exposure becomes provisional after releasing pricing, setting the stage for binding product terms.  

## Binding Product Terms  
- Product terms, such as product_term_template_standard_amortization, are bound to cases for both primary and secondary parties.  
- Confirming exposure profiles commits party exposure, marking them ready for offer assembly.  

## Assembling Loan Offers  
- Loan offer packages are assembled once both parties are ready, linking provisional primary and secondary exposures.  
- Finalizing packages requires re-attaching pricing and confirming readiness, completing the offer process.  

## Managing Approvals and Funding  
- Parties are approved for execution, setting ready flags and linking to loan offer packages.  
- Structuring instances require approval resources, but limited slots (e.g., 3 approvals for 4 entities) may cause conflicts.

## Finalizing Structuring Instances  
- Structuring instances like inst1 require security documents to be instantiated and bound, using templates like mortgage_deed, once the package is allocated and finalized.  
- The process involves validating documents, ensuring pricing is attached, and confirming no primary tranche flag, marking documents as bound to the instance.  

## Managing Pricing Adjustments  
- Pricing adjustments, such as override_1, are attached to structuring instances and committed via the primary path, requiring documents bound and product terms attached.  
- Committing adjustments sets pricing_adjustment_committed, ensuring readiness for external approvals without tranche flags.

## Handling External Approvals  
- External approvals, like external_approval_record_1, attach to structuring instances such as inst1, setting them ready for final checks without tranche flags.  
- Finalizing structuring instances without execution methods requires readiness and no execution requirement, setting a ready flag for activation.  

## Releasing Resources for Reuse  
- Resources like external approvals, pricing adjustments, and product terms can be released from one instance (e.g., inst1) to become available for others (e.g., inst2).  
- Activation of an instance releases approval resources and funding, but other attached elements like pricing remain until explicitly released.  

## Activating New Instances  
- New instances, like inst2, require registering, assigning approval resources, and re-attaching necessary elements like pricing after releasing them from prior instances.  
- Security documents, such as guarantees, must be available, and the process mirrors inst1 but accounts for limited shared resources like external approvals.  

## Managing Resource Constraints  
- Limited resources, such as a single external approval record, necessitate sequential activation and release to handle multiple instances.  
- The process avoids using internal staging records when following the external approval path, focusing on resource availability.

## Confirming Exposure Profiles  
- Primary and secondary exposure profiles are provisionally assigned, requiring pricing and product terms attached to the party.  
- Confirming exposure profiles sets readiness for both primary and secondary parties, committing exposure without negating provisional status.  

## Assembling Loan Offer Packages  
- Assembling requires both primary and secondary exposures provisional and committed, with the package available for allocation.  
- The process links the package to exposures and sets it as allocated, preparing for finalization without tranche flags.  

## Finalizing and Authorizing Packages  
- Finalizing the package needs it allocated and pricing attached to the primary party, setting it as finalized.  
- After finalization, pricing remains attached, allowing authorization and activation for the primary party.  

## Sequencing Resource Releases  
- Product terms can be released from cases after confirming exposure profiles, ensuring availability for reuse.  
- Proper sequencing ensures resources like pricing and product terms are freed post-finalization for subsequent instances.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
